facts.fact: Shakespeare wrote in the English language.
facts.sourceText: Shakespeare remains arguably the most influential writer in the English language, and his works continue to be studied and reinterpreted.

facts.fact: William Shakespeare was born and raised in Stratford-upon-Avon, Warwickshire.
facts.sourceText: Shakespeare was born and raised in Stratford-upon-Avon, Warwickshire.

facts.fact: Shakespeare's private life has not been extensively documented.
facts.sourceText: Few records of Shakespeare's private life survive; this has stimulated considerable speculation about such matters as his physical appearance, his sexuality, his religious beliefs and even certain fringe theories as to whether the works attributed to him were written by others.

facts.fact: England is often referred to as the national poet's homeland.
facts.sourceText: He is often called England's national poet and the "Bard of Avon" or simply "the Bard".

facts.fact: he began a successful career in London
facts.sourceText: Sometime between 1585 and 1592 he began a successful career in London as an actor, writer, and part-owner ("sharer") of a playing company called the Lord Chamberlain's Men, later known as the King's Men after the ascension of King James VI of Scotland to the English throne.

facts.fact: Stratford was the place where a person retired at age 49.
facts.sourceText: At age 49 (around 1613) he appears to have retired to Stratford, where he died three years later.
